# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build Workflow

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      build_commands:
        description: 'Custom shell commands to run during the build process'
        required: true
        default: 'echo "No commands provided"'
        
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Validate input
        id: validate_input
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}" =~ [^a-zA-Z0-9\ \_\-\.\/\(\)\"\'\&\|] ]]; then
            echo "Invalid command detected. Only safe characters are allowed."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Run custom build commands
        run: |
          eval "${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}" || exit 1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
